message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_13-22-54_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Failing test: "should bring a dead cell to life when it has exactly 3 live neighbors (reproduction)"
Expected behavior: nextGeneration([[0,0],[1,0],[0,1]]) result should contain [1,1] (dead cell with 3 live neighbors becomes alive)
Current error: Expected result to contain [1,1] but result was [[0,0],[1,0],[0,1]]
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_13-22-54_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts

Implement the reproduction rule: any dead cell that has exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive. The simplest approach: for each live cell, also check its dead neighbors as candidates. A dead candidate cell becomes alive if it has exactly 3 live neighbors.

Make sure all 6 tests pass after this change.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the reproduction rule with minimal code to make the failing test pass.

content.text:
All 6 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added a candidate-based reproduction step. After computing survivors, iterate over each live cell's dead neighbors, collect them as candidates, and add to the result any candidate with exactly 3 live neighbors.
**Result**: All 6 tests now pass (2 todo remaining).
**Approach**: This follows the suggested algorithm directly: for each live cell, also check its dead neighbors as candidates. Reuses the existing `countLiveNeighbors` and `key` helpers, keeping the change focused on the new behavior. Hardcoding wasn't viable here since the test demands actual reproduction logic, but the implementation is the simplest direct expression of the rule.

Relevant file:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_13-22-54_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
